main.cues: Feet straight, light breath, small range.
main.name: Standing Tandem Stance Shifts
main.description: Heel-to-toe stance → shift weight forward and back → maintain balance. 1. Stand with one foot directly in front of the other (straight line). The heel of the front foot touches the toes of the back foot. 2. Slowly shift weight forward, pressure moves from back foot → front foot. 3. Shift weight backward. 4. Switch foot positions.

warmup.cues: Tall spine, quiet foot, hips level.
warmup.name: Weight-Shift to Single-Leg Hover
warmup.description: Shift weight → lift the other foot → balance. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart. 2. Slowly shift body weight onto the right leg, slow and intentional. 3. Once fully loaded, gently lift the left foot just off the floor, knee slightly bent. 4. Hold 3 sec. 5. Reset and switch legs.

main.cues: Tall spine, hips square, exhale into slow stretch.
main.name: Standing Hamstring Sweep
main.description: Step forward → sweep the hands toward the toes → return. 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, spine long. 2. Step your right foot forward, heel stays on the ground, toes pulled upward, front knee straight. 3. Push your hips back and gently hinge forward over the front leg, keep the back straight. 4. Sweep the arms down toward the toes in a smooth motion. 5. Return to standing and reset. 6. Switch.

warmup.cues: Bounce from the ankles, small jumps.
warmup.name: Dynamic Ankle Jumps
warmup.description: Quick ankle bounce → small jumps → stay light on the feet. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart, knees soft. Core lightly braced. 2. Rise onto the balls of your feet and perform small, quick jumps using mainly the ankles. Keep the knees only slightly bent and the torso upright. Land softly on the balls of the feet, maintain a light, rhythmic bouncing motion.

main.cues: Elbows back and down. Open the chest. Core braced.
main.name: Neck–Shoulder Stretch
main.description: Hands at forehead → open the elbows → squeeze the shoulder blades. 1. Stand or sit tall. Place your hands lightly in front of the forehead, palms facing outward. 2. Inhale, begin to pull the elbows down and back. Rotate the palms outward as the chest opens. 3. Exhale while squeezing the shoulder blades together and gently drawing the head straight back. 4. Neck long and shoulders away from the ears. 5. Hold 5 sec. 6. Return & repeat.

warmup.cues: Long spine, knee tracks, gentle depth.
warmup.name: Standing Figure-4 Hip Opener
warmup.description: Cross the ankle → sit back into the hip → stay tall and balanced. 1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and engage your core. 2. Shift your weight onto one leg. Lift your opposite leg and cross the ankle over the knee, creating a “figure-4” shape. 3. Sit your hips back, like a mini single-leg squat, while keeping your spine long. 4. Hold the top of the shin or keep hands in prayer position for balance. 5. Reset and switch legs.

main.cues: No jaw clench, neck long, blades down/back.
main.name: Chin Tucks + Scapula Squeezes
main.description: Tuck the chin back → squeeze the shoulder blades → stand tall. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width, spine long. 2. Perform a chin tuck (NOT a chin down), gently pull your head straight back, as if making a double-chin. 3. While maintaining the chin tuck. Pull your shoulder blades inward and slightly downward. Think: “put shoulder blades in your back pockets”. 4. Hold 1 sec & reset. 5. Repeat.

warmup.cues: Neck long, ribs stacked, no shrug.
warmup.name: Arms Circles
warmup.description: Arms out → make small circles → keep shoulders relaxed. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart, draw shoulder blades down and slightly back. 2. Lift arms to the side at about shoulder height (or slightly lower if needed). 3. Make small, slow circles with the arms, shoulder blades, stay quiet and stable. 4. Reset and reverse the motion.

main.cues: Slow roll. Tall posture. Reach without arching.
main.name: Heel to Toe Roll + Reach
main.description: Rise onto toes → reach arms forward → lower with control. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart. Arms relaxed at sides. 2. Shift weight slightly forward. Lift heels and rise onto the balls of the feet. 3. As you rise, reach both arms forward and slightly upward (to shoulder or overhead height). 4. Bring arms back down as you return to neutral. 5. Repeat.

warmup.cues: Neck long, ribs down, low effort.
warmup.name: Chin Tucks + Scapula Squeezes
warmup.description: Tuck the chin back → squeeze the shoulder blades. 1. Stand tall with your spine neutral. Gently draw your head straight back (like making a double chin). Keep the chin parallel to the floor, not tilted down. 2. Scapular Squeeze. With the chin tucked, pull your shoulder blades back and slightly down. Imagine pinching a small object between your shoulder blades. 3. Hold 3 sec. 4. Reset and repeat.

main.cues: Rotate through the ribs. Hips steady. Control the return
main.name: Split Stance + Chop
main.description: Split stance → chop diagonally across the body → control the core and return. 1. Split stance, feet hip-width apart (not on a tight line). 2. Shift body weight forward onto the front leg. Clasp hands together and raised above shoulder height. 3. Move the hands diagonally down across the body toward the opposite hip. Rotation comes from the upper body. 4. Slowly bring hands back to the starting position. 5. Repeat and switch legs.

warmup.cues: Hips back, shins near vertical, ribs down.
warmup.name: Hamstring Scoop
warmup.description: Step forward → toes up → scoop the floor. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart, knees soft. 2. Step the right foot forward, placing the heel on the floor with toes lifted, the front leg straight. 3. Reach both hands down and forward, making a scooping motion toward the toes. 4. Return to standing. 5. Repeat on the other leg.

main.cues: Hips square. Control the center, no weight shift.
main.name: Single Leg Toe-Tap Matrix
main.description: Balance on one leg → tap the free foot in different directions → stay stable. 1. Stand tall on the left leg, knee softly bent. Hands on hips or out to sides. 2. Using the right foot, lightly tap the floor in different directions: 12 o’clock, 3 and 6  o’clock. Stance knee stays aligned over toes. Slow and controlled. 3. Repeat and switch sides.

warmup.cues: Rotation comes from the thoracic (chest) spine, not the lower back.
warmup.name: Standing T-Spine Rotation
warmup.description: Rotate through ribs → keep hips quiet. 1. Stand tall, feet shoulder-width apart. Arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height, forming a T. 2. Rotate your torso to the right, arms stay wide and level, chest turns with the arms. 3. Slowly rotate back to center. 4. Rotate to the left. 5. Continue alternating.

main.cues: Hips square, ribs stacked, eyes forward.
main.name: Half-Kneeling Bracing
main.description: Half-kneeling stance → brace the core → stay tall and stable. 1. Kneel on the left knee. Right foot forward, flat on the floor. Hips square, torso upright. 2. Take a slow inhale through the nose, as you exhale, gently tighten the abdominal wall, imagine preparing for a light punch. Create tension around the entire trunk. Arms relaxed or hands on hips, maintain balance. 3. Hold up to 3 sec. 4.  Reset and repeat. 5. Switch legs.

warmup.cues: One segment at a time. Chin first, head last. Stack slowly.
warmup.name: Segmented Spine Wave Flow
warmup.description: Ripple down through the spine → flow forward → rebuild the spine one segment at a time. 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees soft, arms relaxed by sides. 2. Long exhale 8 sec, curl down (flexion wave). Slowly round the upper spine, mid-back, lower back. Let arms hang naturally. 3. Start the inhale. Gently initiate from the pelvis. Stack the spine upward one segment at a time. 4. Reset. 5. Repeat.

main.cues: Hips square. Control the center, no weight shift.
main.name: Single Leg Toe-Tap Matrix
main.description: Balance on one leg → tap the free foot in different directions → stay stable. 1. Stand tall on the left leg, knee softly bent. Hands on hips or out to sides. 2. Using the right foot, lightly tap the floor in different directions: 12 o’clock, 3 and 6  o’clock. Stance knee stays aligned over toes. Slow and controlled. 3. Repeat and switch sides.

warmup.name: Standing Arm Swings + Calf Raises
warmup.description: Swing arms → rise onto toes → move tall and rhythmic. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart. 2. Calf Raise, press through the balls of the feet, lift the heels off the floor. 3. Swing both arms forward and up or swing them back and forth naturally. 4. Continue rising and lowering in a steady rhythm.

main.cues: Hips back. Weight on front leg. Long spine.
main.name: Staggered Stance Hinge
main.description: Staggered stance → hinge at the hips → keep the spine long. 1. Stand with right foot forward, left foot back. Back foot on toes, 70% of weight on the front leg. Arms hanging naturally or hands on hips 2. Hinge. Push hips straight back, maintain neutral spine. Front knee softly bent. 3. Brace the core, maintain hinge position 3 sec. Feel load in the front glute and hamstring. 4. Return to tall standing and repeat. 5. Switch legs.

warmup.cues: Long spine, knee tracks, gentle depth.
warmup.name: Standing Figure-4 Hip Opener
warmup.description: Cross the ankle → sit back into the hip → stay tall and balanced. 1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and engage your core. 2. Shift your weight onto one leg. Lift your opposite leg and cross the ankle over the knee, creating a “figure-4” shape. 3. Sit your hips back, like a mini single-leg squat, while keeping your spine long. 4. Hold the top of the shin or keep hands in prayer position for balance. 5. Reset and switch legs.

main.cues: Slow roll. Tall posture. Reach without arching.
main.name: Heel to Toe Roll + Reach
main.description: Rise onto toes → reach arms forward → lower with control. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart. Arms relaxed at sides. 2. Shift weight slightly forward. Lift heels and rise onto the balls of the feet. 3. As you rise, reach both arms forward and slightly upward (to shoulder or overhead height). 4. Bring arms back down as you return to neutral. 5. Repeat.

warmup.cues: Hips back, shins near vertical, ribs down.
warmup.name: Hamstring Scoop
warmup.description: Step forward → toes up → scoop the floor. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart, knees soft. 2. Step the right foot forward, placing the heel on the floor with toes lifted, the front leg straight. 3. Reach both hands down and forward, making a scooping motion toward the toes. 4. Return to standing. 5. Repeat on the other leg.

main.cues: Rotate through the ribs. Hips steady. Control the return
main.name: Split Stance + Chop
main.description: Split stance → chop diagonally across the body → control the core and return. 1. Split stance, feet hip-width apart (not on a tight line). 2. Shift body weight forward onto the front leg. Clasp hands together and raised above shoulder height. 3. Move the hands diagonally down across the body toward the opposite hip. Rotation comes from the upper body. 4. Slowly bring hands back to the starting position. 5. Repeat and switch legs.

warmup.cues: Head leads down, pelvis leads up, breathe slow.
warmup.name: Segmented Spine Wave Flow
warmup.description: Ripple down through the spine → flow forward → rebuild the spine one segment at a time. 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees soft, arms relaxed by sides. 2. Long exhale 8 sec, curl down (flexion wave). Slowly round the upper spine, mid-back, lower back. Let arms hang naturally. 3. Start the inhale. Gently initiate from the pelvis. Stack the spine upward one segment at a time. 4. Reset. 5. Repeat.

main.cues: Slow roll. Tall posture. Reach without arching.
main.name: Heel to Toe Roll + Reach
main.description: Rise onto toes → reach arms forward → lower with control. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart. Arms relaxed at sides. 2. Shift weight slightly forward. Lift heels and rise onto the balls of the feet. 3. As you rise, reach both arms forward and slightly upward (to shoulder or overhead height). 4. Bring arms back down as you return to neutral. 5. Repeat.

warmup.cues: Keep chest lifted and breathe with rhythm.
warmup.name: March in Place
warmup.description: Walk without moving forward. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart. 2. Lift the right knee to a comfortable height (shin roughly vertical). 3. Let arms swing naturally opposite the legs (right knee → left arm forward). 4. Reset 5. Continue alternating in a smooth rhythm.

main.cues: Hips square, ribs stacked, eyes forward.
main.name: Half-Kneeling Bracing
main.description: Half-kneeling stance → brace the core → stay tall and stable. 1. Kneel on the left knee. Right foot forward, flat on the floor. Hips square, torso upright. 2. Take a slow inhale through the nose, as you exhale, gently tighten the abdominal wall, imagine preparing for a light punch. Create tension around the entire trunk. Arms relaxed or hands on hips, maintain balance. 3. Hold up to 3 sec. 4.  Reset and repeat. 5. Switch legs.

warmup.cues: Relax shoulders, gentle rhythm, quiet feet.
warmup.name: Dynamic Ankle Bounce
warmup.description: Stay tall → bounce lightly through the ankles → quick and elastic. 1. Stand upright, feet hip-width apart. 2. Rise onto the ball of the one foot. 3. Quickly switch. The weight alternates rapidly from one ankle to the other. Small, quick bounces, stay light and springy. Relaxed upper body.

main.cues: Tall spine, hips square, exhale into slow stretch.
main.name: Standing Hamstring Sweep
main.description: Step forward → sweep the hands toward the toes → return. 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, spine long. 2. Step your right foot forward, heel stays on the ground, toes pulled upward, front knee straight. 3. Push your hips back and gently hinge forward over the front leg, keep the back straight. 4. Sweep the arms down toward the toes in a smooth motion. 5. Return to standing and reset. 6. Switch.

warmup.cues: Bounce from the ankles, small jumps.
warmup.name: Dynamic Ankle Jumps
warmup.description: Quick ankle bounce → small jumps → stay light on the feet. 1. Stand tall, feet hip-width apart, knees soft. Core lightly braced. 2. Rise onto the balls of your feet and perform small, quick jumps using mainly the ankles. Keep the knees only slightly bent and the torso upright. Land softly on the balls of the feet, maintain a light, rhythmic bouncing motion.

main.cues: Rotate through the ribs. Hips steady. Control the return
main.name: Split Stance + Chop
main.description: Split stance → chop diagonally across the body → control the core and return. 1. Split stance, feet hip-width apart (not on a tight line). 2. Shift body weight forward onto the front leg. Clasp hands together and raised above shoulder height. 3. Move the hands diagonally down across the body toward the opposite hip. Rotation comes from the upper body. 4. Slowly bring hands back to the starting position. 5. Repeat and switch legs.

warmup.cues: Lengthen back of neck, move slow, no pinching.
warmup.name: Neck Articular Rotations
warmup.description: Draw a slow circle with your nose → move smoothly. 1. Head centered, eyes forward. 2. Flexion (chin down). 3. Side bend. 4. Extension (look up). 5. Complete the full circle smoothly.
